_comment: easy val + hard val, combined — used to gate skill changes on both regression-safety (easy) and headroom (hard)

val.prompt: Build a simple calculator: a display and number/operator buttons that compute results in vanilla JavaScript. Single index.html. Then call done.
Build a live digital clock, centered, that updates every second with vanilla JavaScript and shows the current time. Single index.html. Then call done.

expect.label: the displayed time advances every second

val.prompt: Build a profile card: a circular avatar, a name, a short bio line, and a 'Follow' button. Single index.html. Then call done.

Build a tabbed panel: three tabs labeled 'Overview', 'Features', and 'Pricing', and below them a content area that shows ONLY the selected tab's paragraph. Clicking a tab reveals its panel and hides the others (vanilla JavaScript). Single index.html. Then call done.

expect.label: clicking Pricing changes the visible panel content

val.prompt: Build a text box with a live character counter below it that shows how many characters have been typed and updates as you type (vanilla JavaScript). Single index.html. Then call done.

Build a calculator in a single index.html with vanilla JS: digit buttons and operator buttons +, −, ×, ÷ that respect operator PRECEDENCE (multiply and divide bind tighter than add and subtract), plus = and Clear. So 2 + 3 × 4 = must give 14, not 20. Then call done.

Build a to-do app in a single index.html with vanilla JS: a text input + Add button; each item has a checkbox to complete it and a delete button; and filter buttons 'All', 'Active', 'Completed' that show only the matching items. Then call done.

expect.label: the Completed filter hides the active items

val.prompt: Build a sortable table in a single index.html with vanilla JS: a table of three people — Charlie 30, Alice 50, Bob 10 (columns: name, score) — and a 'Sort by score' button that reorders the rows ascending by score. Then call done.
Build a 3-question multiple-choice quiz in a single index.html with vanilla JS. Each question has selectable options, and a Submit button at the end shows the score formatted as N/3. Then call done.

expect.label: Submit shows a score out of 3

val.prompt: Build a paginated list in a single index.html with vanilla JS: 12 numbered items shown 5 per page, with Prev and Next buttons and a 'Page X of 3' indicator that updates. Then call done.

expect.label: Next advances to the next page of items

val.prompt: Build a to-do app in React as a NO-BUILD single-page app: load React, ReactDOM, and Babel from a CDN and put JSX in a <script type="text/babel"> (Oxy has no bundler). An input plus an "Add" button adds a task to a visible list; clicking a task toggles it done. Mount into <div id="root">. Then call done.

expect.label: adding a task renders it in the list (React-via-CDN actually runs)

val.prompt: Build a reusable Web Component <click-counter>: a custom element that uses shadow DOM and shows a button labeled "Count: 0" which increments the number on each click. Use <click-counter> in index.html. Then call done.

expect.label: clicking increments the counter (custom element + shadow DOM works)

val.prompt: Build a notes app with Supabase auth: an email/password form with Email and Password fields and "Sign In" and "Sign Up" buttons, and (after sign-in) a list of notes with an input to add one. Use the Supabase JS client with the SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY consts left as placeholders. Then call done.
